events.message: No image uploaded, so Vision Agent was skipped and typed ingredients were used directly.

details.reason_summary: Skipping vision avoids unnecessary model calls when the user already typed confirmed ingredients.

events.message: Validating cooking goals, allergies, portions, time, and tools.

details.reason_summary: Serving count, cooking time, allergies, and available tools are checked before planning.

events.message: Generating recipe candidates with RAG references, portion math, cooking details, and rough nutrition.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Stuffed Bell Peppers
directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Cut peppers in half lengthwise. Remove stems, rubs and seeds. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat, swirling to cover bottom. Saut crookneck squash, zucchini, onion and garlic until zucchini is tender-crisp (about 4 minutes). Don't overcook. In a medium bowl, combine rice and cheese. Gently stir into skillet. Stuff pepper halves with vegetable mixture. Place in a 9-inch casserole dish. Carefully pour tomato sauce around peppers. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes. Serves 8.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Spinach-Rice Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb. fresh spinach, washed and chopped
directions: Cook for 40 minutes the brown rice and vegetable broth. Prepare the Cheddar cheese, parsley and fresh spinach while rice is cooking.
After rice has cooked 30 minutes, preheat oven to 350°. Mix cooked rice with the Cheddar cheese mixture adding garlic powder, black pepper and eggs.
Spoon mixture into greased casserole dish.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Tortilla Soup
directions: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot set over medium-high heat. Add the tortillas, chili powder, cayenne pepper, paprika, cumin, garlic and bay leaves and saute for 10 minutes.
Add onion, red bell pepper and cilantro; cook 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes.

recipe_candidates.title: Dinner Stuffed Bell Peppers Skillet
recipe_candidates.description: Everything cooked together in one pan: chicken breast, eggs, cooked rice with simple seasoning, ready in about 30 minutes.

ingredient_amounts.name: chicken breast
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

ingredient_amounts.name: eggs
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

ingredient_amounts.name: cooked rice
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

ingredient_amounts.name: spinach
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

steps: Measure the ingredients: 240 g chicken breast, 2 eggs (about 100 g) eggs, 150 g cooked rice, 160 g spinach for 2 portion(s).
Cut firm vegetables or proteins from chicken breast, eggs, cooked rice, spinach into 1 to 2 cm bite-size pieces so they cook evenly; leave delicate greens larger because they wilt quickly.
Heat a wide pan over medium heat for 2 minutes, then add 1 tablespoon oil per 4 portions.

Add chicken pieces first and keep the pieces in one even layer; cook on medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes per side for 1 to 2 cm pieces, or 7 to 8 minutes per side for whole thicker pieces. Chicken is ready when no pink remains in the center and the juices run clear.
Fold spinach or delicate greens in during the final 60 to 90 seconds only, then season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately or divide into 2 dinner portion(s).

recipe_candidates.title: Dinner Chicken Breast Bowl
recipe_candidates.description: A quick warm bowl built around chicken breast, finished with a splash of something tangy so it tastes fresh, not like leftovers.

ingredient_amounts.name: chicken breast
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

ingredient_amounts.name: eggs
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

ingredient_amounts.name: cooked rice
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 2 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

steps: Measure the bowl base: 240 g chicken breast, 2 eggs (about 100 g) eggs, 150 g cooked rice.
Prep chicken breast first: slice thin if it is firm, chop bite-size if it is tender, or keep it whole only if it is already cooked.
Warm the cooked grain or beans in a covered pan with 2 tablespoons water for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring halfway; add tender vegetables at the end so they soften without collapsing.
Divide into 2 dinner portion(s), then finish each portion with a small splash of lemon juice or vinegar and black pepper.

Add chicken pieces first and keep the pieces in one even layer; cook on medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes per side for 1 to 2 cm pieces, or 7 to 8 minutes per side for whole thicker pieces. Chicken is ready when no pink remains in the center and the juices run clear.
Fold spinach or delicate greens in during the final 60 to 90 seconds only, then season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately or divide into 2 dinner portion(s).

events.message: Validating cooking goals, allergies, portions, time, and tools.

events.message: Generating recipe candidates with RAG references, portion math, cooking details, and rough nutrition.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Stuffed Bell Peppers
directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Cut peppers in half lengthwise. Remove stems, rubs and seeds. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat, swirling to cover bottom. Saut crookneck squash, zucchini, onion and garlic until zucchini is tender-crisp (about 4 minutes). Don't overcook. In a medium bowl, combine rice and cheese. Gently stir into skillet. Stuff pepper halves with vegetable mixture. Place in a 9-inch casserole dish. Carefully pour tomato sauce around peppers. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes. Serves 8.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Spinach-Rice Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb. fresh spinach, washed and chopped
directions: Cook for 40 minutes the brown rice and vegetable broth. Prepare the Cheddar cheese, parsley and fresh spinach while rice is cooking.
After rice has cooked 30 minutes, preheat oven to 350°. Mix cooked rice with the Cheddar cheese mixture adding garlic powder, black pepper and eggs.
Spoon mixture into greased casserole dish.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Tortilla Soup
directions: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot set over medium-high heat. Add the tortillas, chili powder, cayenne pepper, paprika, cumin, garlic and bay leaves and saute for 10 minutes.
Add onion, red bell pepper and cilantro; cook 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Stuffed Bell Peppers
directions: Preheat oven to 375°. Cut peppers in half lengthwise. Remove stems, rubs and seeds. Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat, swirling to cover bottom. Saut crookneck squash, zucchini, onion and garlic until zucchini is tender-crisp (about 4 minutes). Don't overcook. In a medium bowl, combine rice and cheese. Gently stir into skillet. Stuff pepper halves with vegetable mixture. Place in a 9-inch casserole dish. Carefully pour tomato sauce around peppers. Bake, uncovered, for 30 minutes. Serves 8.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Spinach-Rice Casserole
ingredients: 1 lb. fresh spinach, washed and chopped
directions: Cook for 40 minutes the brown rice and vegetable broth. Prepare the Cheddar cheese, parsley and fresh spinach while rice is cooking.
After rice has cooked 30 minutes, preheat oven to 350°. Mix cooked rice with the Cheddar cheese mixture adding garlic powder, black pepper and eggs.
Spoon mixture into greased casserole dish.

retrieved_recipe_references.title: Tortilla Soup
directions: Heat vegetable oil in a large pot set over medium-high heat. Add the tortillas, chili powder, cayenne pepper, paprika, cumin, garlic and bay leaves and saute for 10 minutes.
Add onion, red bell pepper and cilantro; cook 5 minutes.
Add tomatoes and cook 5 minutes.

candidate.title: Meal Prep Chicken Breast Bowl
candidate.description: A quick warm bowl built around chicken breast, finished with a splash of something tangy so it tastes fresh, not like leftovers.

ingredient_amounts.name: chicken breast
ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 4 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

ingredient_amounts.notes: Calculated for 4 portion(s): per-portion amount multiplied by requested portions.

steps: Measure the bowl base: 480 g chicken breast, 4 eggs (about 200 g) eggs, 300 g cooked rice.
Prep chicken breast first: slice thin if it is firm, chop bite-size if it is tender, or keep it whole only if it is already cooked.
Warm the cooked grain or beans in a covered pan with 2 tablespoons water for 4 to 6 minutes, stirring halfway; add tender vegetables at the end so they soften without collapsing.
Divide into 4 meal prep portion(s), then finish each portion with a small splash of lemon juice or vinegar and black pepper.

steps: Measure the ingredients: 480 g chicken breast, 4 eggs (about 200 g) eggs, 300 g cooked rice, 320 g spinach for 4 portion(s).
Cut firm vegetables or proteins from chicken breast, eggs, cooked rice, spinach into 1 to 2 cm bite-size pieces so they cook evenly; leave delicate greens larger because they wilt quickly.
Heat a wide pan over medium heat for 2 minutes, then add 1 tablespoon oil per 4 portions.

Add chicken pieces first and keep the pieces in one even layer; cook on medium-high heat for 4 to 5 minutes per side for 1 to 2 cm pieces, or 7 to 8 minutes per side for whole thicker pieces. Chicken is ready when no pink remains in the center and the juices run clear.
Fold spinach or delicate greens in during the final 60 to 90 seconds only, then season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately or divide into 4 meal prep portion(s).
